headlines.title: Are you lying down? These 10-minute toe and core exercises can help improve balance
headlines.description: Balance is a strong indicator of how well you’re aging. Researchers in Tokyo found that a daily series of core and toe exercises helped with balance and agility.

headlines.title: Rectal cancer deaths rising rapidly among millennials: 'It's a medical crisis'
headlines.description: Deaths from rectal cancer are rising rapidly among younger adults, an alarming trend that is confounding scientists trying to understand why millennials are so hard-hit.

headlines.title: What does it take to protect brain health? 10 habits to start now
headlines.description: Brain health is of paramount importance to nearly all Americans, yet few are aware of the latest science on how to nurture it

headlines.title: Pancreatic cancer mRNA vaccine shows lasting results in an early trial
headlines.description: Nearly all of the patients who responded to the a personalized mRNA pancreatic vaccine are still alive six years later.

headlines.title: Non-Smoker Lung Cancer Survivor Shares Important Message
headlines.description: Lung cancer in non-smokers is on the rise, causing more deaths among women than breast, ovarian, and uterine cancer combined, according to the National Institute of Health. Shira Kupperman Boehler, avid runner, mother of four, and non-smoking lung cancer survivor, joins TODAY along with lung surgeon Dr. Jeffrey Port to share her story and raise awareness about the importance of lung cancer screenings, particularly among non-smokers, which she attributes to her survival.

headlines.title: Wedding Dress Industry Disrupted by Weight Loss Drug Craze
headlines.description: A growing number of Americans are turning to weight loss drugs as they prepare for their wedding day. Now, the often dramatic results of these drugs are shaking up the wedding dress industry with more women saying yes to the dress much closer to their big day, having to guesstimate their sizes in anticipation of additional weight loss. NBC’s Emilie Ikeda reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Best Ways to Use Sunscreens and Protect From Skin Cancer
headlines.description: Dr. Michelle Henry, dermatologist and a board member at the Melanoma Research Foundation, sits down with TODAY’s Savannah Guthrie to break down what you need to know about skin safety from the sun. She shares the most effective sunscreens to use, how often you need to reapply, how to check your skin for melanoma and more.

headlines.title: ‘Jump Rope Queen’ Talks Staying Young at 82 Years Old
headlines.description: At the sprightly age of 82, Annie Judis, former model and TV star, is now known as the “Jump Rope Queen” after picking up a jump rope eight years ago and never looking back. In her new lease on life, Judis has found jumping rope helps her stay happy and healthy and is inspiring others to do the same through motivational videos that have gained her almost 200,000 Instagram followers and led her to write a new children’s book. With 12 Guinness World Records under her belt, Annie Judis join

headlines.title: I’ve tested dozens of workout headphones: These are the best
headlines.description: I tested over a dozen workout headphones from brands like Apple, Beats, and Shokz to find our top picks for running, biking, the gym and more.

headlines.title: Expert-approved walking pads that help you sit less and move more
headlines.description: Fitness trainers share the benefits of using walking pads daily for up to 60 mins. and safety tips. Plus, shop the best expert-approved walking pads.

headlines.title: The best spin shoes of 2026, according to spin instructors
headlines.description: Our experts share their favorite spin shoes, plus what to look for in fit and feel. Shop picks from Shimano, Tiem, Peloton and more.

headlines.title: Amazfit vs. Garmin: Can a $100 fitness tracker compete with an $1,100 one?
headlines.description: The $100 Amazfit Active 2 can’t be as good as the $1,100 Garmin Fenix 8, right? I tested both for over a month to find out.
